name: ismtp
title: ismtp

commands.name: ismtp

arguments.description: Imports a list of SMTP servers for testing.
arguments.raw: -f <import file>	Imports a list of SMTP servers for testing.

arguments.description: Specifies both the sender's and recipient's email address.
arguments.raw: --sr <email>		Specifies both the sender's and recipient's email address.

arguments.description: The sender's first and last name.
arguments.raw: -S <sndr name>		The sender's first and last name.

arguments.description: The recipient's first and last name.
arguments.raw: -R <rcpt name>		The recipient's first and last name.

arguments.description: Specifies both the sender's and recipient's first and last name.
arguments.raw: --SR <name>		Specifies both the sender's and recipient's first and last name.

arguments.description: Enable SMTP user enumeration testing and imports email list.
arguments.raw: -e <file>	Enable SMTP user enumeration testing and imports email list.

arguments.description: Specifies enumeration type (1 = VRFY, 2 = RCPT TO, 3 = all).
arguments.raw: -l <1|2|3>	Specifies enumeration type (1 = VRFY, 2 = RCPT TO, 3 = all).

arguments.description: Creates "ismtp-results" directory and writes output to
arguments.raw: -o		Creates "ismtp-results" directory and writes output to

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# ismtp -h
 
Error: option -h requires argument
 
---------------------------------------------------------------------
 iSMTP v1.6 - SMTP Server Tester, Alton Johnson (alton.jx@gmail.com)
---------------------------------------------------------------------

Usage: ./iSMTP.py <OPTIONS>
 
Required:
 
 	-f <import file>	Imports a list of SMTP servers for testing.
 				(Cannot use with '-h'.)
 	-h <host>		The target IP and port (IP:port).
 				(Cannot use with '-f'.)
 
Spoofing:
 
 	-i <consultant email>	The consultant's email address.
 	-s <sndr email>		The sender's email address.
 	-r <rcpt email>		The recipient's email address.
 	   --sr <email>		Specifies both the sender's and recipient's email address.
 	-S <sndr name>		The sender's first and last name.
 	-R <rcpt name>		The recipient's first and last name.
 	   --SR <name>		Specifies both the sender's and recipient's first and last name.
 	-m			Enables SMTP spoof testing.
 	-a			Includes .txt attachment with spoofed email.
 
SMTP enumeration:
 
 	-e <file>	Enable SMTP user enumeration testing and imports email list.
 	-l <1|2|3>	Specifies enumeration type (1 = VRFY, 2 = RCPT TO, 3 = all).
 			(Default is 3.)
 
SMTP relay:
 
 	-i <consultant email>	The consultant's email address.
 	-x			Enables SMTP external relay testing.
 
Misc:
 
 	-t <secs>	The timeout value. (Default is 10.)
 	-o		Creates "ismtp-results" directory and writes output to
 			ismtp-results/smtp_<service>_<ip>(port).txt
 
Note: Any combination of options is supported (e.g., enumeration, relay, both, all, etc.).

examples.command:
ismtp -f smtp-ips.txt -e /usr/share/wordlists/metasploit/unix_users.txt

 ---------------------------------------------------------------------
  iSMTP v1.6 - SMTP Server Tester, Alton Johnson (alton.jx@gmail.com)
 ---------------------------------------------------------------------

 Testing SMTP server [user enumeration]: 192.168.1.25:25
 Emails provided for testing: 109

 Performing SMTP VRFY test...

 [-] 4Dgifts ------------- [ invalid ]
 [-] EZsetup ------------- [ invalid ]
 [+] ROOT ---------------- [ success ]
 [+] adm ----------------- [ success ]

binary_metadata.file_type: Python script, ASCII text executable, with very long lines (406)

apt_metadata.description:
SMTP user enumeration and testing tool
Test for SMTP user enumeration (RCPT TO and VRFY), internal spoofing, and
relay.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use ismtp because ### ismtp.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `./iSMTP.py <OPTIONS>` as documented upstream for ismtp.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair ismtp with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `ismtp --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install ismtp. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `./iSMTP.py <OPTIONS>`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### ismtp.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install ismtp
Install with: sudo apt install ismtp
